title: Configure cron in America/New_York with proper tz dependency note
prompt: The config in `test_fixtures/cron_config_example.exs` uses `timezone: "America/New_York"` but does not document that this requires the `tz` (or `tzdata`) library as a separate dependency. On app boot, Oban crashes with a cryptic 'unknown time zone' error because the core Elixir `Calendar.ISO` module has no non-UTC zones. Rewrite `config/runtime.exs` so: (1) the timezone remains `America/New_York`, (2) the sub-minute cron entry `"*/30 * * * * *"` is removed (Oban cron has 1-minute resolution), (3) a comment at the top of the config block explicitly states: 'Requires `{:tz, "~> 0.26"}` in mix.exs deps and `Calendar.put_time_zone_database(Tz.TimeZoneDatabase)` in application.ex.start/2'. Keep the other entries. Write to `config/runtime.exs`.
tier_rationale: Requires niche knowledge of Elixir time zone support + Oban plugin requirements. Most Sonnet outputs leave the tz dependency implicit and never mention `put_time_zone_database/1`.
research_citation: Oban.Plugins.Cron HexDocs — 'To use a timezone other than the default of Etc/UTC you must have a timezone database like tz installed and configured.'
